summary: High‑energy 1983 show with a scorching St. Stephen, rare China Doll and a rocking pre‑drums run, captured in a solid matrix recording.
review: The concert is remembered as one of the hottest Dead shows, driven by unstoppable stage energy and standout performances of St. Stephen, Wang Dang Doodle, Jack Straw, Birdsong, Bertha and a rare Phil‑sung China Doll. Reviewers note only occasional vocal raggedness but praise the overall power, especially in the second set. A matrix (or audience) mix is recommended to fully enjoy the ambience and clarity of this night.

ai_rating.rationale: A single detailed 5‑star review highlights an exceptional performance with many standout songs; recording is praised as a must‑hear matrix, giving a high combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer urges listening to the matrix or audience mix, indicating the capture is clear enough despite some vocal raggedness; overall sound is lively and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with multiple highlights; second set steps up dramatically, featuring a rock‑inspired pre‑drums run and sustained high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil sang lead on China Doll, a rare occurrence that impressed the crowd.
